meta.title: GEONYX THERM | Thermal Resistant Floor System
meta.description: GEONYX THERM – geopolymer flooring for zones with extreme heat, sparks and thermal shock. Suitable for foundries, furnaces and hot industrial processes.

hero.description: Mineral heat-resistant flooring for metallurgy, energy and food industries – from shock freezing to molten metal. Class A1fl and temperature range -40°C / +1200°C.

diagnostics.title: FROM -40°C TO +1200°C. THE SYSTEM DOES NOT FAIL.
diagnostics.subtitle: 8 thermal and operational scenarios that standard coatings cannot handle.

d1.title: STEAM THERMAL SHOCK
d1.problem: Steam cleaning at 120°C on a cold floor causes epoxy coatings to crack and delaminate instantly.
d1.solution: GEONYX THERM has a thermal expansion coefficient close to concrete and maintains bond integrity through repeated steam–cold cycles.

d2.title: MOLTEN METAL AND SPARKS
d2.problem: Weld splatter and foundry processes (700°C+) burn through polymer floors and create fire hazards.
d2.solution: Mineral matrix resistant to direct contact with fire and molten metal – no ignition, no deformation.

d3.title: SHOCK FREEZING
d3.problem: At -40°C standard coatings become brittle and crack under vibration and mechanical load.
d3.solution: GEONYX THERM retains load-bearing capacity and micro-elasticity under cryogenic conditions and frequent freeze–thaw cycles.

d4.title: FIRE SAFETY AND SMOKE
d4.problem: In a fire, synthetic floor coverings emit dense toxic smoke and poisonous gases.
d4.solution: GEONYX THERM is class A1fl – non-combustible, contributes nothing to fire and produces no smoke or toxic fumes.

d5.title: HOT OIL AND FATS
d5.problem: Boiling fats (180°C) and oils in the food industry corrode concrete and polymer layers.
d5.solution: Dense mineral structure that prevents fat penetration and remains stable at high temperatures and chemical exposure.

d6.title: EXOTHERMIC PROCESSES
d6.problem: Chemical reactions in reactors and baths can raise the temperature to hundreds of degrees within seconds.
d6.solution: GEONYX THERM is designed for short-duration temperature peaks up to 1200°C, without loss of adhesion to concrete.

d7.title: STERILISATION AND CIP
d7.problem: Autoclaving, pressurised hot water and aggressive cleaning agents destroy joints and weak zones.
d7.solution: Monolithic joint-free system resistant to CIP regimes, steam washing and disinfection in clean rooms and food facilities.

d8.title: SPARK GENERATION IN ATEX ZONES
d8.problem: Mechanical sparks on impact in gas or dust zones can trigger an explosion.
d8.solution: Non-sparking configuration of GEONYX THERM – the surface generates no mechanical sparks on impact or friction.

anatomy.sectionSubtitle: Interactive layer model – from the substrate to the working surface.

h1.title: 1. LOAD-BEARING CONCRETE
h1.subtitle: Prepared substrate
h1.desc: Structurally verified concrete, ground or milled, with corrected joints and defects for resistance to dynamic and thermal loads.

h2.title: 2. ADHESION ZONE
h2.subtitle: Thermally compatible bond
h2.desc: Special mineral bond with an expansion coefficient close to concrete – ensuring zero delamination under thermal shock.

h3.title: 3. THERM CORE
h3.subtitle: Heat-resistant layer
h3.desc: Primary GEONYX THERM layer that absorbs extreme temperature peaks and mechanical load without brittle fracture.

h4.title: 4. FUNCTIONAL FINISH
h4.subtitle: Anti-slip and hygiene
h4.desc: R12/R13 texture for safety, easy cleaning and compatibility with food, steam and aggressive cleaning agents.

engineering.title: LABORATORY EVIDENCE
engineering.subtitle: Independent tests in accredited laboratories – full protocols available in TDS.
engineering.block1note: GEONYX THERM expands at the same rate as concrete and generates no stress in the adhesion layer.
engineering.block2note: Comparison of maximum temperature range under stable adhesion.
engineering.block3note: Mineral matrix with no combustible components – fire class A1fl per EN 13501-1.

r1.title: MONOLITHIC ADHESION UNDER THERMAL SHOCK
r1.desc: No delamination during transition from 500°C to ambient temperature – verified in fire simulations and real production conditions.

r2.title: CLASS A1fl (NON-COMBUSTIBLE)
r2.desc: No contribution to fire, no smoke and no toxic gases – safe for evacuation and fire services.

r3.title: HACCP COMPATIBILITY
r3.desc: Suitable for food production facilities – no hazardous components, easy cleaning, resistance to hot oil.

r4.title: CRYOGENIC RESISTANCE
r4.desc: Operates at -40°C without change in strength – for cold stores, cooling tunnels and cryogenic storage.

r5.title: ATEX / NON-SPARKING
r5.desc: Special configurations that generate no mechanical sparks – for zones with flammable gases, dust and ammunition.

financial.title: TCO UNDER THERMAL LOAD
financial.subtitle: Maintenance cost comparison under extreme temperature conditions.
financial.quote: FLOORS IN METALLURGY ARE REPLACED EVERY 3–5 YEARS.

roi.title: INVESTMENT AUDIT
roi.subtitle: 20-year cycle comparison for thermally loaded zones.

applications.title: WHERE IT IS USED
applications.subtitle: Facilities with thermal aggression, fire hazard or extreme temperature cycles.

a1.title: Metallurgy and Foundry
a1.desc: Foundries, casting zones, furnace areas and zones with molten metal splatter.

a2.title: Food and Beverage Industry
a2.desc: Production facilities with hot oil, steam and aggressive cleaning (HACCP).

a3.title: Energy and Nuclear Power
a3.desc: Turbine halls, boiler rooms and fire-rated zones requiring class A1.

a4.title: Aviation Industry
a4.desc: Hangars with ATEX requirements and fire-resistant flooring.

a5.title: Chemical and Petroleum Industry
a5.desc: ATEX zones, reactors and areas with ignition risk.

a6.title: Cold Storage Facilities
a6.desc: Cryogenic warehouses at -30°C/-40°C with heavy forklift traffic.

a7.title: Pharmaceutical Industry
a7.desc: Sterilisation zones, autoclaving and CIP programmes.

a8.title: Defence Industry
a8.desc: Blast-protected bunkers, ATEX rooms for ammunition.

process.title: EXECUTION PROCESS
process.subtitle: Controlled execution under active thermal conditions.

p1.title: Thermal Zone Audit
p1.desc: Mapping of temperature peaks, cycles and operations by zone.

p2.title: THERM Configuration Selection
p2.desc: Selection of exact thickness and system (standard, ATEX or cryogenic).

p3.title: Substrate Preparation
p3.desc: Mechanical profiling, levelling of irregularities and thermal activation of adhesion.

p4.title: Layer Application
p4.desc: Application following the thermal-technological protocol with temperature and adhesion monitoring.

p5.title: Quality Control and Testing
p5.desc: Verification of adhesion, hardness and levelness. Thermal test where required.

p6.title: Documentation and Handover
p6.desc: TDS package, ATEX/HACCP certificates and maintenance instructions.

offer.title: THERM TENDER PACKAGE (B2B / B2G)
offer.desc: Complete documentation for thermally loaded zones in metallurgy, food and energy industries.
offer.qualityDesc: All documents comply with HACCP, ATEX and EN 13501-1 requirements.
offer.doc1: TDS – Technical specifications and thermal resistance
offer.doc2: Fire class A1fl – Certificate and protocol
offer.doc5: Expansion joint and thermal zone details
offer.doc6: Tender specification for thermally loaded zones

cta.title: FIRE SAFETY. THERMAL RESISTANCE. MINIMUM DOWNTIME.
cta.desc: Describe the thermal load of your zones – we will propose a THERM configuration, ATEX/HACCP documentation and TCO analysis.

footerBand.label: TECHNOLOGY PARTNER FOR CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE
footerBand.desc: GEONYX THERM provides thermal protection where standard floor coverings burn, melt or delaminate.
